title: cinchona ovata var. rufinervis
intro: Cinchona ovata var. rufinervis is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the cinchona or Jesuit's bark tree. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its high content of quinine, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Cinchona ovata var. rufinervis include treating fever, reducing inflammation, and preventing the contraction of malaria. The plant is often prepared for herbal use through the process of distillation, yielding a medicinal oil or by being made into a decoction, a liquid extract that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
